name: 선진기업의 AX내재화 사례
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

This conversation involved a person working at LG PRI on the EXG team in a role they describe as "Creative Solution Provider" (CSP), focused on designing and implementing AX (AI Transformation) internalization across the organization. The person is working toward a 2026 mission with four KPI axes: AI utilization (6 education courses), Physical AI Tech Leader pool (LV3+ 30 people), productivity improvement (+10%), and Well-Being improvement (+15%) with Pulse Check participation at 85%. The conversation progressed from exploratory research through to concrete deliverable creation, covering the full arc of AX internalization strategy design.

The person asked Claude to research global enterprise AX internalization cases, then progressively deepen the analysis into infrastructure-tool linkage, tipping point theory, and LG PRI-specific implementation. Key frameworks developed include the "Tipping Point" model (three simultaneous conditions: accessibility, felt value, social normalization), a four-phase 2026 roadmap (Q1 foundation → Q2 first success experiences → Q3 social contagion → Q4 tipping point), and an AX Maturity Index (ATI) using minimum-value logic across four measurement dimensions. The conversation also covered Pulse Check AX maturity question integration (6 monthly + 5 quarterly items), Vibe-Coding education design centered on same-day completion experiences, AX Champion appointment ceremony design as identity ritual based on van Gennep's rites of passage theory, team-level AX maturity dashboard with 12 indicators, Physical AI Tech Leader Q3 execution plan across three parallel tracks, and HR SaaS PoC documentation framing the entire LG PRI experiment as startup validation data.

Multiple deliverables were created during the conversation: a 7-slide AX CoE kickoff PowerPoint (navy/teal palette), an 8-page HR SaaS PoC documentation Word template with dual-layer structure (internal record + SaaS asset), an interactive 4-phase roadmap widget, an interactive tipping point explorer widget, an AX internalization framework SVG diagram, a 5-sheet Excel use case library with 30 initial use cases across 7 business domains with dropdown validation, and a 7-page comprehensive task execution plan Word document structured around the image-based reporting format the person shared. The person explicitly requested content enrichment beyond the image format, and the final Word document was structured with six chapters: background/necessity, four-pillar task framework, annual roadmap, KPIs, execution governance, and expected outcomes. The person's stated long-term vision is to convert the LG PRI AX internalization journey into a HR SaaS platform startup PoC, with the phrase "psychology becomes code, philosophy becomes algorithm, HR experience becomes SaaS product" serving as the north star throughout.

좋은 자료들이 모였습니다. 이제 CSP의 2026 미션 4개 축과 직접 연결되는 방식으로 선진기업 사례를 분석해서 제안드리겠습니다.

---

## 선진기업의 AX 내재화 사례 — 그리고 LG PRI가 배울 수 있는 것들

---

### 들어가며: 왜 대부분의 AX는 내재화에 실패하는가

먼저 냉정한 숫자 하나를 직시할 필요가 있습니다. 2025년 McKinsey 보고서에 따르면 92%의 기업이 AI 투자를 늘릴 계획이지만, 실제로 AI가 조직 전반의 측정 가능한 성과를 만들어내는 '완전한 운영 통합' 단계에 도달한 기업은 단 1%에 불과합니다. 이것이 내재화 문제의 본질입니다. 도입은 쉽고, 내재화는 어렵습니다.

IDC 조사에 따르면 전체 AI 프로젝트의 88%가 파일럿 단계에 머물러 전사 규모로 나아가지 못합니다. 33개 AI 이니셔티브 중 성공적으로 확산되는 것은 통계적으로 4개에 불과합니다. 기술의 문제가 아닙니다. 조직과 사람의 문제입니다.

그렇다면 그 1%에 속하는 기업들은 무엇을 다르게 했는가. 그 질문에 답하는 네 가지 사례가 있습니다.

---

### Case 1. Microsoft — "자사가 먼저 쓰는 기업" 전략

Microsoft의 AX 내재화가 설득력을 갖는 이유는 단순합니다. 그들은 Copilot을 판매하기 전에 자신들이 먼저 실험했습니다. Microsoft는 2023년 11월부터 자사 임직원에게 Copilot을 배포하기 시작했고, 2024년 3월까지 전 세계 모든 직원과 협력사에 접근권을 부여했습니다 — 글로벌 엔터프라이즈 규모에서 최초로 Copilot을 전사 배포한 기업이 된 것입니다.

내재화 방법론으로는 학습 생태계 설계가 돋보입니다. Marketing AI practitioner hub를 통해 마케터들이 AI를 꾸준히 실천할 수 있는 정기적인 접점을 만들었고, MCAPS Academy는 영업 인력이 AI 학습에 계속 참여하도록 뉴스레터와 내부 커뮤니케이션을 활용했습니다. 엔지니어링 팀은 The Garage를 통해 AI 프로젝트 성과를 공유하는 Show-and-Tell 세션을 운영했습니다.

이것이 LG PRI에 주는 시사점: 단순 교육이 아니라 '반복 접점'의 설계가 내재화의 핵심입니다. CSP의 Vibe-Coding 교육 6개 과정 신설은 도입부이고, 그 이후에 마케터 허브처럼 실천 커뮤니티가 따라와야 합니다.

---

### Case 2. IKEA — "30,000명을 위한 AI 리터러시 아키텍처"

2024년 출범한 IKEA의 AI 리터러시 이니셔티브는 30,000명의 직원과 500명의 리더를 대상으로 AI 도구를 책임감 있게 활용하는 능력을 기르는 것을 목표로 합니다. 규모만 인상적인 것이 아닙니다. 설계 철학이 다릅니다.

레벨 구조가 정교합니다. 모든 직원을 위한 AI Fundamentals 기초 과정, Responsible AI·Mastering Gen AI·Algorithmic Training for Ethics 등의 전문 프로그램, 리더십 집중 세션인 AI Exploration Days, 그리고 ML/AI 석·박사급 고급 인재를 위한 Accelerator Programme까지 — 단계별로 서로 다른 대상에게 맞춤화된 학습 경로를 제공합니다.

CSP의 "모두의 AX" 트랙 A/B, Lv.1~4 체계와 거의 정확히 동형 구조입니다. IKEA는 이것을 이미 30,000명 규모로 실행했고, 그 경험이 증명하는 것은 레벨 분화 없이 일괄 교육으로는 내재화가 일어나지 않는다는 사실입니다.

---

### Case 3. Airbus — "엔지니어 10,000명의 기술 내재화"

Airbus는 약 10,000명의 엔지니어를 GitHub 등의 도구로 교육하는 데 투자했고, 그 결과 항공기 설계 시뮬레이션 속도가 40% 가속되었습니다. 이 사례가 Physical AI Tech Leader 육성에 주는 함의는 명확합니다. 도구 교육 자체보다 도구를 통해 달성하고자 하는 구체적인 업무 성과물이 먼저 정의되어야 한다는 것입니다. Airbus가 "GitHub 교육"을 한 것이 아니라 "시뮬레이션 속도 향상을 위해 GitHub을 가르친" 것처럼, LG PRI의 Physical AI Tech Leader 육성도 Skill Ontology가 단순 역량 목록이 아니라 특정 현장 문제의 해결 경로로 설계되어야 합니다.

---

### Case 4. BCI (British Columbia Investment Management) — "생산성 측정의 정밀화"

CSP의 KPI인 "생산성 10% 개선"을 달성한 실제 사례가 있습니다. BCI는 Microsoft 365 Copilot과 Azure 생태계를 활용하여 수작업을 자동화한 결과, Copilot 사용자의 84%에서 10~20%의 생산성 향상이 이루어졌고, 직무 만족도는 68% 향상되었습니다. 내부 감사 보고서 작성 시간은 30% 단축되었으며, 8,000개의 설문 코멘트 분석에 한 달이 소요되던 처리 시간이 대폭 줄었습니다.

특히 설문 코멘트 분석 자동화 사례는 CSP의 Pulse Check 시스템과 직결됩니다. 조직 건강도 데이터를 분기에 한 번 수동으로 처리하는 방식이 아니라, AI가 상시적으로 텍스트 응답을 분석하고 패턴을 감지하는 구조로 가야 한다는 것을 BCI 사례는 보여줍니다.

---

### 선진기업 사례를 관통하는 세 가지 내재화 패턴

조각난 사례들을 하나의 프레임으로 통합하면, 성공한 AX 내재화 기업들은 공통적으로 세 가지 조건을 갖추고 있습니다.

**첫 번째는 내부 챔피언 네트워크입니다.** 성공한 조직들은 내부 변화 주도자가 이니셔티브를 이끌게 하고, 모범 사례를 공유하는 커뮤니티 오브 프랙티스를 만들며, 도입 성과를 게임처럼 경쟁하게 하고, 초기 성과를 기념합니다. 상명하달식 롤아웃이 아니라 내부 전파자가 스스로 동료를 설득하는 구조입니다.

**두 번째는 측정 가능한 성과 연결입니다.** AI 상위 성과 기업들 — AI 활용으로 EBIT의 5% 이상을 창출하는 조직들 — 은 대담한 목표, 워크플로우 재설계, 빠른 확산, 그리고 동종 기업보다 현저히 높은 AI 역량 투자라는 공통 특성을 보입니다.

**세 번째는 심리적 안전감의 선행 구축입니다.** PwC와 WEF 공동 보고서에 따르면 조기 채택자들은 더 넓은 실행에 앞서 소규모 그룹에서 GenAI 솔루션을 개발하고 테스트하는 것의 중요성을 강조했으며, 리스크 관리와 '인간 참여 프로세스'에 집중했습니다. 기술 도입 전에 "이것은 당신의 역할을 위협하지 않는다"는 신뢰 기반이 먼저 만들어져야 내재화가 일어납니다.

---

### LG PRI에 대한 제언

이 모든 사례를 종합하면, LG PRI의 AX 내재화 전략에 다음 세 가지를 보완 제안합니다.

**Pulse Check에 AI 기반 텍스트 분석 레이어 추가**: BCI 사례처럼, 설문 응답의 정량 집계를 넘어 자유응답 텍스트에서 패턴을 추출하는 AI 레이어를 설계하면 "Well-Being 15% 개선"이라는 KPI를 단순 점수 변화가 아니라 원인 진단 기반의 개입으로 연결할 수 있습니다.

**AX 챔피언 네트워크의 공식화**: Microsoft와 IKEA 모두 내부 커뮤니티 오브 프랙티스를 공식 제도로 운영합니다. Vibe-Coding 교육 이수자 중 우수자를 단순 수료생이 아닌 AX 챔피언으로 공식 임명하고, 이들이 팀 단위 전파자로 기능하게 설계하면 교육 효과의 반감기를 늦출 수 있습니다.

**Physical AI Tech Leader 육성의 성과 중심 재정의**: Airbus처럼, "Lv.3 이상 30명 Pool"이라는 수량 목표를 "특정 현장 문제를 AI로 해결한 경험이 있는 엔지니어 30명"으로 재정의하면 그 자체가 내부 레퍼런스 스토리가 되고 이후 확산의 기반이 됩니다.

내재화란 결국 기술이 일상 언어가 되는 과정입니다. 사람들이 AI를 사용하는 것이 특별한 일이 아닌, 당연한 업무 방식이 되는 그 임계점까지 가는 것 — 선진기업들의 사례가 공통으로 향하는 방향이 그것입니다.

content.title: AI Transformation: A Complete Strategy Guide for 2025 | Databricks Blog
content.text:
They create communities of practice sharing best practices, run competitions gamifying adoption, and celebrate early wins demonstrating value. Training should span multiple levels.

Gen AI usage jumped from 55% in 2023 to 75% in 2024. Survey respondents across industries report using AI in at least one business function, with marketing, sales, and customer service leading adoption rates. Despite widespread experimentation, most organizations struggle with scaling AI beyond pilots. The challenge isn't technological—it's organizational. Companies treating gen AI as technology deployment rather than business transformation leave pilot projects stranded, unable to deliver measurable impact that leadership teams demand. AI high performers—organizations attributing 5% or more EBIT impact to AI use—share common traits: bold ambitions, redesigned workflows, faster scaling, and significantly higher investment in AI capabilities compared to their peers. Many organizations underestimate the scope required to put data and AI to work effectively.

Successful organizations have internal change agents leading initiatives from within. They create communities of practice sharing best practices, run competitions gamifying adoption, and celebrate early wins demonstrating value. Training should span multiple levels. Basic literacy helps everyone understand what AI can and can't do. Functional training teaches using tools relevant to roles. Advanced training develops capabilities in technical staff building and maintaining systems. Remember: people want to be successful. When you provide clear paths—training, support, recognition—most embrace transformation enthusiastically, seeing AI as amplifying capabilities rather than replacing them. The move to cloud computing unlocked flexibility but introduced complexity. When evaluating technologies for your stack, remember less is often more. Every additional tool creates integration challenges, increases learning curves, and adds maintenance overhead.

content.title: The State of AI Adoption in Global Enterprises: 2025 Benchmark Report with 300+ Company Survey
content.text:
Invest in talent and infrastructure: Build data platforms, get the right compute capacity, develop AI fluency across teams. Foster capable culture: Encourage experimentation, continuous learning, cross-functional collaboration.

Integration into the ASEAN digital cooperation framework opens access to shared markets and resources — giving Vietnamese enterprises the ability to scale innovation beyond domestic borders. Private investment and international collaboration add fuel to this fire. If executed well, Vietnam can anchor the region’s next wave of digital transformation. Here’s the action plan for companies that want to win in the AI era: Define strategic clarity: Focus on use-cases tied to measurable outcomes rather than chasing AI for the sake of novelty. Invest in talent and infrastructure: Build data platforms, get the right compute capacity, develop AI fluency across teams. Foster capable culture: Encourage experimentation, continuous learning, cross-functional collaboration. Embed governance and ethics: From day one, build in data stewardship, model audits, bias mitigation, privacy controls.

For Vietnam in particular: The country is gaining momentum. A recent article notes that Vietnam’s 2024 Government AI Readiness Index ranked it among the top five in ASEAN, and globally it placed third in “AI trust” and fifth in “AI acceptance.” · Vietnam has a clear policy in place: the National Strategy on Research, Development and Application of Artificial Intelligence to 2030 was established to build a mature AI ecosystem, align with socio-economic goals, and embed ethics and governance at the core. The environment is favourable. The ambition is real. The next step is execution. Here are some of the standout metrics that show both traction and room for growth: A study across e-commerce merchants in six ASEAN countries found that Vietnam and Indonesia each had an AI adoption rate of 42%, surpassing Singapore and Thailand (39%). In Vietnam, a survey found 94.3% of engineers used AI for writing code, 70% used it to write documents, and 62.9% applied it to testing code — the highest reg

Globally, AI is scaling rapidly. According to market trackers, the global AI sector is projected to reach approximately US $254.5 billion in 2025, growing at a ~36.9% CAGR toward 2031. Similarly, the long-term productivity opportunity of AI is estimated at US $4.4 trillion in added growth potential from corporate use cases. What this means: AI is not a marginal innovation any longer. It’s a foundational technology driving large-scale transformation. In terms of enterprise penetration, the recent McKinsey & Company “State of AI” survey shows that 78% of companies now use AI in at least one business function. Within that: about 71% of respondents say they regularly use generative AI (gen AI) in at least one function. However, only about 1% of firms describe themselves as “mature” in AI deployment (i.e., AI fully embedded, producing major business outcomes).

For all the positive signals, several barriers are holding back scaled AI transformation. If enterprises want to capture value, they must address these. Shortage of AI-specific talent: A large number of IT graduates in Vietnam lack deep AI expertise; many firms report a talent gap (55%) as the strongest barrier. Workforce transition risk: Up to 85% of repetitive-task workers may face displacement or need re-skilling as AI expands — which introduces organisational and social risk. Leadership and cultural lag: Only about 24% of companies feel confident in their workforce’s AI capabilities; just 12% of large enterprises say they have a comprehensive AI strategy. Infrastructure and compute constraints: Many AI initiatives in Vietnam remain at the “basic use-case” level, rather than advanced or innovative deployment. Fragmented data and silos: High-quality, integrated data remains rare, undermining model reliability and scalability.

content.title: AI in Digital Transformation Strategy 2025: 6 Key Trends for Large Companies | TTMS
content.text:
Many large companies have successful AI pilot implementations behind them – prototypes of models or limited rollouts, e.g. in one department. However, for AI to truly change business, it cannot remain an isolated experiment. The AI ​​strategy should include a plan to move from PoC (proof of concept) to production use on a large scale, in all places where the technology brings value. And this can be a problem – as IDC research shows, as many as 88% of AI projects get stuck at the pilot stage and do not go into production on a company-wide scale. In other words, statistically only 4 out of 33 AI initiatives manage to successfully develop globally. The reasons can be various: lack of clear business goals for the project, insufficient data or infrastructure quality, difficulties in integrating the solution with existing systems, as well as a shortage of talent (lack of MLOps, data science experts). In 2025, large organizations are therefore focusing on AI scalability and maintenance.

The applications are very wide: automation of creating marketing content, generating personalized offers for customers, creating chatbots that can conduct natural dialogue, supporting R&D departments (e.g. generating and testing new product concepts), and even assistance in programming (an “artificial programmer” suggesting code). Today, 71% of organizations declare regular use of generative AI in at least one area of ​​activity (up from 65% at the beginning of 2024). This means that generative models have very quickly moved from the phase of curiosity to practical implementations in business. For leaders of digital transformation, generative AI is a double challenge: on the one hand, a huge opportunity for innovation, and on the other – the need for caution and ethics (more on that in a moment). Trends indicate that in the coming years, companies will build their own generative models specialized in their domain (e.g. a model that will generate a financial report based on company data

Read more · Not long ago, artificial intelligence in education was mainly portrayed as a promise — a tool meant to ease teachers’ workload, accelerate the creation of materials, and help tailor learning to students’ needs. Today, however, it increasingly becomes a source of questions, concerns, and debate. The more frequently AI appears in classrooms and on e-learning platforms, the more the conversation shifts from technology itself to responsibility. We know that AI can generate teaching materials. But an increasingly common question is: who is responsible for their content, quality, and impact on learning? At the center of this discussion stands the teacher — not as a user of a new tool, but as a guardian of the educational relationship, trust, and ethics. This is where the topic of ethics emerges. content.title: AI strategies that are working - I by IMD - AI Maturity Index 2025
content.text:
AI maturity is no longer defined by experimentation. In 2025, the organizations pulling ahead are those that treat AI not as a siloed initiative but as a catalyst for reinvention.

Technology - This sector dominates the AI Maturity Index, with firms not only deploying AI but setting the pace for its global advancement, building foundational infrastructure and releasing developer tools, APIs, and platforms that power AI transformation across sectors. If 2024 was the year companies experimented with GenAI, 2025 is the year organizations are racing to scale. Most business leaders accept that AI is going to revolutionize many aspects of working life, but many are nervous about how to harness it to maximum advantage – and even more concerned about being left behind. AI maturity is no longer defined by experimentation. In 2025, the organizations pulling ahead are those that treat AI not as a siloed initiative but as a catalyst for reinvention. The ranking evaluates how effectively the world’s top 300 companies are leveraging AI to reshape strategy, operations, and competitive advantage.

Automotive - The sector continues its transformation from mechanical manufacturing to intelligent mobility. content.title: From hype to real business impact: How to lead AI transformation in 2025
content.text:
According to a 2025 McKinsey report, 92% of companies plan to increase their AI investment, but only 1% have achieved full operational integration, where AI actively drives measurable outcomes across functions and informs broader business strategy.

AI success is based on smart investments. According to EY’s 2024 research, organizations allocating at least 5% of their total budgets to AI are significantly more likely to report positive returns across productivity, operations, and innovation. But investment alone isn’t enough. The report also found that two-thirds of business leaders say infrastructure limitations are slowing them down, and 83% believe stronger data systems would accelerate adoption and support scalable business models. To support sustainable growth, organizations are shifting focus to the essentials: better data architecture, stronger governance, and enabling employees to move away from administrative tasks and focus on high-value initiatives. For example, Zalando, a European fashion retailer, has integrated generative AI to expedite content production for marketing campaigns.

Talent: A cross-functional group leads internal AI skill-building · Governance: We have documented policies for reviewing and managing AI systems · Culture: Employees are encouraged to explore and propose AI use cases · Tooling: We can prototype and deploy AI workflows without waiting on IT · Your responses can guide the next steps in your AI journey, but because benchmarks vary by industry and goals, a deeper assessment may be helpful. Reaching AI maturity means more than having the right tools. It also means having the right systems, teams, and strategy in place. According to a 2025 McKinsey report, 92% of companies plan to increase their AI investment, but only 1% have achieved full operational integration, where AI actively drives measurable outcomes across functions and informs broader business strategy. This maturity gap presents a strategic advantage.

Half of organizations cite privacy and security concerns as top blockers, especially in early-adoption stages. In some cases, there is the challenge of shadow AI, or employees using unapproved tools that create visibility and governance issues. In highly regulated industries like finance, insurance, and healthcare, AI workflows must align with standards like HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2, and internal controls. Organizations use monday work management to meet those requirements with permission controls, audit trails, and customizable processes, without slowing innovation. Secure deployment is supported through the platform’s permission-based processes, admin oversight, and enterprise-grade compliance. Every automation, integration, and AI flow is auditable and easy to govern at scale. Introducing new tools often meets internal pushback, with teams disengaging when tools are introduced without clear value.

Monitor early-stage pilots through live dashboards and shared KPIs. With customizable reports and up-to-date insights, leaders can track what’s working, communicate outcomes, and build internal momentum. As early-stage results take hold, the focus shifts to building scalable, compliant systems. As organizations grow more confident, templates, admin permissions, and audit tools make it easier to expand adoption securely. Whether deploying AI in ops, support, or marketing, monday work management provides governance at every level. AI should fit naturally into everyday work. Built-in capabilities within monday work management make that possible: AI Assistant: Generates content, summarizes updates, and suggests next steps. Smart replies: Helps service teams resolve requests faster with recommended responses. 200+ automations: Trigger actions, assign owners, and reduce manual work across processes.

This requires carefully framing AI adoption as a general-purpose tool and efficiency-improving technology, not as a threat to their jobs. AI is the equivalent of the internet or computer literacy skills in the late 1990s; it’s an emerging technology that will soon become a standard part of the white-collar toolkit, and workers can’t afford to ignore it. ... Microsoft was one of the first Fortune 500 companies to embrace ADKAR for change management. The tech giant used ADKAR to quickly diagnose customer pain points (and customer service bottlenecks), and quickly get employee buy-in for new processes and frameworks. The Microsoft 365 Customer Success team used ADKAR to create awareness of new tools and features, gather customer feedback to understand communication gaps, and get their customer success staff, consulting teams, and end users on the same page.

Maintaining a Competitive Advantage — Your competitors are embracing AI right now. To stay competitive and maintain employment levels, organizations must adopt AI-driven technologies today. This will help your business thrive in AI and give employees job security in a tough job market. This is a strong message for stakeholders and internal leaders. No matter which messaging leaders choose, it’s important to ensure that storytelling is personalized for current employees and stakeholders. Tying AI adoption to the company’s (and employees’) long-term financial success is also essential. Numerous companies have launched their AI transformation with employee messaging (aka awareness), including: Microsoft: Created awareness of how AI can improve workflows and free employees from boring tasks. “Using AI to streamline or automate repetitive, mundane tasks can allow your employees to dive into more complex, creative and ultimately more valuable work.” ·

One of the most effective strategies is identifying internal champions who can promote this narrative and proactively address employee fears. According to Forbes, “Success will belong to leaders who embrace AI without losing the human touch, instill trust in an age of uncertainty, and reskill their workforce for a future no one can fully predict.” · Finally, transparency is the key to success in this step. Clearly explain the reasoning behind AI transformations to employees. Tie business outcomes and C-suite strategies to this new technology. In addition, employees should be involved in the decision-making process as early as possible. This will give them a sense of ownership in the process and confidence in the future. Here’s how some of the world’s biggest companies are creating employee desire for AI, and tying employee well-being with the new technology: ...

Reinforce AI successes by celebrating small wins. Congratulate and reward employees who launch successful pilot projects. Consider tying these wins to performance bonuses, annual raises, and promotions. Use internal newsletters and win wires to announce these achievements to the company. In addition, leaders can create a culture of innovation by scaling pilot projects into larger initiatives. Employees love to see their efforts celebrated (and recognized company-wide). This encourages other workers to innovate and build their own success stories. Here are a few examples from successful enterprises that have scaled AI pilot projects into full-blown AI-powered strategies. ... content.title: AI Integration and Upskilling
content.text:
Providing resources for experimentation and learning is also key. By guiding your workforce towards some initial use cases to upskill can help them become comfortable with the technology and ignite their curiosity, empowering them to explore its potential further. For example, data from our PwC AI and productivity report in collaboration with the World Economic Forum, found that early adopters emphasised the importance of developing and testing GenAI solutions in small groups before wider implementation, focusing on risk management and ‘human-in-the-loop’ processes. Similarly, fostering a culture of curiosity, experimentation, and exploration can empower your workforce to uncover the potential of GenAI and AI agents, thereby driving innovation and growth. The sooner you begin thinking this way — and transforming your operating model to plan, train, and manage a blended digital and human workforce — the better positioned you will be to capitalise on AI.

Similarly, our latest PwC EMEA Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey, which surveyed 24,600 employees from 29 sectors in 27 countries and was published in June 2024, found that 56% of workers believe GenAI will boost their efficiency, with 70% feeling optimistic that emerging technology will continue to create opportunities to learn new skills. There is no denying that AI, and more specifically GenAI, is transforming how we live and work, and has the potential to contribute to economic and productivity growth by creating efficiencies in how much time employees spend on lower-value tasks to engage in higher value-strategic activities (PwC AI and productivity report). As highlighted in PwC’s 2025 AI Predictions, the rules of competition are evolving rapidly, with the traditional workforce on the brink of transformation. Even those at the forefront of AI — transforming our organisation and guiding clients through their own AI journeys — cannot fully predict what lies ahead.

Similarly, 41% expect to integrate AI into their core business processes, but just 31% say that they plan to integrate it into workforce and skills over the same period (PwC CEO Survey). While integrating AI into core business processes is a significant step forward, it is equally important to address the concern that technology implementation and upskilling should go hand in hand. This ensures that employees are well-equipped to work with AI and deliver on its promises. Without proper training and skill development, the potential benefits of AI may not be fully realised, and the workforce may struggle to adapt to new technologies. According to our PwC EMEA Workforce Hopes & Fears Survey (published in June 2024), only 9% of employees across the cluster said they use GenAI daily to perform their work, with regular use varying by generation, country and industry.

Providing resources for experimentation and learning is also key. By guiding your workforce towards some initial use cases to upskill can help them become comfortable with the technology and ignite their curiosity, empowering them to explore its potential further. For example, data from our PwC AI and productivity report in collaboration with the World Economic Forum, found that early adopters emphasised the importance of developing and testing GenAI solutions in small groups before wider implementation, focusing on risk management and ‘human-in-the-loop’ processes. Similarly, fostering a culture of curiosity, experimentation, and exploration can empower your workforce to uncover the potential of GenAI and AI agents, thereby driving innovation and growth. The sooner you begin thinking this way — and transforming your operating model to plan, train, and manage a blended digital and human workforce — the better positioned you will be to capitalise on AI.

content.title: Bridging the AI skills gap with opportunities for learning | Microsoft Education Blog
content.text:
Innovative collaboration – Pearson and Microsoft will launch a strategic collaboration aimed at helping people build AI proficiency and technical skills through new AI credentials and certifications.

The partnership extends the efforts of both Microsoft and Pearson to provide AI skilling to people across the globe. In 2024, Microsoft and its partners trained and certified over 23 million people in digital skills. Pearson launched its Generative AI Foundations certification to equip professionals and students with the essential skills needed to work with generative AI technologies. Additionally, organizations around the world use Pearson VUE, along with Pearson’s AI-powered Faethm capability, and Credly badging to diagnose, assess, and certify skills. Curious about additional ways to develop AI literacy and build AI skills? Get started today and join the Microsoft AI Skills Fest. Registration is open now to engage in deep dives, experiential content, hackathons, and practical sessions that will enhance your AI skills over 50 days of discovery and learning, starting April 8, 2025.

The 2024 Work Trend Index Annual Report from Microsoft and LinkedIn also found that 66% of leaders say they wouldn’t hire someone without AI skills. As we celebrate National AI Literacy Day in the US on March 28 this year, it’s clear that no one company will likely be able to meet the opportunities of tomorrow. We believe it’ll take innovative partnerships to meaningfully impact the lives of people around the world with AI literacy and skills development. Transforming the future of learning with AI skilling: Watch the video · Microsoft and Pearson, the world’s lifelong learning company, announced a strategic collaboration to help address one of the top challenges facing organizations globally: skilling for the era of AI. The partnership will focus on providing employers, workers, and learners with AI-powered products and services to help prepare the current and future workforce across industries for the evolving landscape of work in an AI-powered economy.

Microsoft and Pearson are addressing the challenges and opportunities around reskilling at the ASU-GSV Summit in San Diego, US, April 6-9, 2025. The summit is dedicated to the scaled innovations in the delivery of education and workforce skills that are critical to creating a world in which all people have equal access to the future. At ASU-GSV, Microsoft Corporate Vice President of Worldwide Learning, Jeana Jorgensen, will join Pearson President of Workforce Skills, Vishaal Gupta, for a discussion on transforming skills development and talent planning for the AI era. They’ll talk about how rapid intervention is needed or we risk the AI skills gap becoming a skills chasm, threatening the ability of individuals and organizations to thrive in an AI-powered future. I’ll be also joining Vishaal and Jeana for a discussion at ASU-GSV on skilling for the AI era.

Personalized learning at scale – Pearson will power its trusted and world-renowned content, assessment, upskilling, and certification services with Microsoft Azure cloud computing and AI infrastructure. This partnership will help Pearson further scale AI and technology capabilities across the business, expanding personalized learning and AI-enabled services to millions of learners, at different stages in their learning journey across the globe. Innovative collaboration – Pearson and Microsoft will launch a strategic collaboration aimed at helping people build AI proficiency and technical skills through new AI credentials and certifications. Additionally, Pearson and Microsoft will collaborate on a series of copilots, agents, and AI tools targeted at helping people develop skills—such as English language learning—and identify skills gaps seamlessly while they work.

content.title: AI Corporate Training: The $44.6B Future of E-Learning by Virtasant
content.text:
Businesses that embrace AI corporate training can enhance learning opportunities, creating better learning and development systems that improve productivity and help workers upskill into better resources. According to the World Economic Forum’s Future of Jobs Report 2023, 60% of workers will require extensive training before 2023. However, identifying the exact skills that employees lack can pose a challenge. By exploring the most in-demand jobs and expanding roles, we can identify the skills organizations currently seek. Nearly all of the fastest-growing 10 jobs worldwide are centered around technology. Yet, businesses don’t simply have to hire new personnel to fill these in-demand roles. Erik Brynjolfsson, a Professor at the Stanford Institute of Human-Centred AI suggests that businesses can focus on identifying skill gaps and reskilling their current workforce.

By using personalized learning on the OTM system, Schneider Electric was able to upskill its internal employees. One team saw productivity enhancements of 2x based on this new knowledge and skills. The internal AI corporate training platform also facilitates connections between senior and junior staff in their business. Connecting people based on interests or aspirations forges powerful mentorship opportunities. It can also analyze an employee’s future career aspirations and point them toward training opportunities that would help them advance. Commenting on the power of AI in education and learning platforms, Nick Day from Roundtable Learning stated that: “Studies have shown that early adopters of artificial intelligence (AI) for their L&D programs have seen, on average, a 57% increase in efficiency, 55% increase in safety, 52% increase in productivity, and 47% complexity reduction.” ·

Within organizations, employees are actively looking to expand upon their skills. In 2022, the main focus on workplace learning and development was upskilling and reskilling. 59% of employees sought to learn new skills. Yet, businesses must approach their diverse workforces with a one-size-fits-all education model. These currently existing educational structures need to consider how distinct individuals learn differently. AI corporate training allows businesses to create hyper-personalized training modules, facilitating a more effective learning pathway that teaches necessary skills appropriately to the employee. AI models can rapidly create synthesized lessons, adapting content to fit an employee’s preferred learning method. In November 2023, Deloitte released Project 120, centering AI’s ability to personalize learning and development opportunities in this project.

The World Economic Forum predicts corporate learning and development upskilling can create 5.3 million jobs net by 2030. Teaching workers new skills increases their value as employees enhance productivity, increasing business profits. According to McKinsey, the vast majority of the global economic value that AI technology provides comes directly from improvements to worker productivity. Researchers predict that by using AI tools, workers could add 7.9 trillion to the global economy. Yet, with the ability to adapt to emerging technologies and incorporate them into workflows, these gains will be noticed. Jim Hemgen, Talent Development Director at Booz Allen Hamilton, comments on the vital nature of AI corporate training. After investing more in corporate training and AI technology to provide advanced learning opportunities, Hemgen stated that: “We are investing in key technology and partnerships to arm our employees with the capabilities they need for years.

Businesses that embrace AI corporate training can enhance learning opportunities, creating better learning and development systems that improve productivity and help workers upskill into better resources. According to the World Economic Forum’s Future of Jobs Report 2023, 60% of workers will require extensive training before 2023. However, identifying the exact skills that employees lack can pose a challenge. By exploring the most in-demand jobs and expanding roles, we can identify the skills organizations currently seek. Nearly all of the fastest-growing 10 jobs worldwide are centered around technology. Yet, businesses don’t simply have to hire new personnel to fill these in-demand roles. Erik Brynjolfsson, a Professor at the Stanford Institute of Human-Centred AI suggests that businesses can focus on identifying skill gaps and reskilling their current workforce.

By following these steps, you’ll create early momentum, build internal expertise, and identify the AI solutions most likely to drive meaningful impact at scale. ... The first tangible benefits of AI: efficiency gains, time and cost savings, and quality improvements, and an internal talent pool emerging, paving the way to scale successful solutions. ... Find out how we’re embracing AI at Microsoft with new AI certifications. To scale and integrate AI solutions across your organization, move beyond pilot projects by deploying AI solutions directly into production and embedding them within core business workflows.

Start by identifying areas where agentic AI can drive real business value. Empower domain experts within your teams to become Agent Leaders—individuals who can design, oversee, and govern agent ecosystems at scale. Align your AI strategy with forward-looking industry insights and best practices—sources like the 2025 Annual Work Trend Index: The Frontier Firm Is Born offer invaluable guidance for responsible AI adoption and organizational transformation. Recognize that the impact will be significant. Industry analysts such as Gartner predict that by 2028, about a third of enterprise applications will feature agentic AI capabilities and over 15% of daily work decisions will be handled by AI agents. Fully autonomous workflows powered by multiple agents are the future of work. To get ahead, foster a culture of experimentation. Host hackathons, pilot agentic AI prototypes, and develop governance frameworks that ensure responsible management of these emerging technologies.

To build an AI-ready workforce and foster a culture of innovation, prioritize company-wide training and upskilling programs that elevate AI literacy at every level. Establish a Center of Excellence and empower “AI champions” within teams to drive adoption and celebrate meaningful impact. Encourage open collaboration—share code, best practices, and project outcomes across your organization—to accelerate learning and scale success. By breaking down silos and enabling employees to experiment with intelligent solutions, you’ll create the environment needed for sustained growth and enterprise-wide transformation. In Microsoft Digital, we are not just training our employees to use AI, we are empowering them to co-create the future of their roles. When employees are empowered to build and govern their own agents, that is when transformation truly scales.

Develop AI expertise through training and development. Upskilling your teams empowers them to navigate the rapid advances of AI, drive innovation, and ensure your organization stays competitive as agentic AI transforms workflows and business outcomes across every industry. Align AI initiatives with strategic business goals. Ensuring AI initiatives align with business goals maximizes impact and positions your organization to succeed in the rapidly evolving world of agentic AI. Implement ethical AI practices based on Microsoft’s Responsible AI Principles. Adopting ethical AI practices builds trust, ensures responsible innovation, and prepares your organization to navigate the evolving landscape as AI becomes central to business operations and decision-making. ... Read our guide for deploying and driving adoption of Microsoft 365 Copilot. ... Learn how we’re using good governance to ride the wave of agents washing over Microsoft.

content.title: The future of work is here: Transforming our employee experience with AI - Inside Track Blog
content.text:
Delivers a secure, customizable gateway to internal communications and resources, seamlessly integrated into Microsoft Teams to enhance employee engagement without adding new infrastructure.

Long before the COVID-19 pandemic, the experts at Gallup saw that employee engagement—defined as the involvement and enthusiasm of employees in their work and workplace—was extremely low. In fact, in 2009 only 12% of employees globally indicated that they were engaged at work. While that number steadily improved over the next 15 years, by 2024 only 21% of employees globally indicated they were engaged in their workplace. (Gallup, 2025) Interestingly, the numbers are not uniform globally. At one end of the spectrum, in the United States, Canada, Latin America, and the Caribbean, 31% of employees indicate they are engaged in work. At the other end of the spectrum, only 13% of European workers indicate they are engaged. (Gallup, 2025) No matter where a region falls on this spectrum, it’s not very promising for companies who want to attract, retain, and develop the best employees.

Delivers a secure, customizable gateway to internal communications and resources, seamlessly integrated into Microsoft Teams to enhance employee engagement without adding new infrastructure. ... Provides privacy-protected, data-driven insights that help improve productivity and well-being while ensuring compliance with organizational and regulatory standards. ... Centralizes learning content from Microsoft, LinkedIn, and third-party providers into Teams, simplifying deployment and governance of upskilling initiatives. ... Empowers corporate communicators to manage multi-channel campaigns with analytics and targeting, all within the Microsoft 365 compliance boundary. ... Fosters community and connection through social experiences in Teams, with enterprise-grade compliance and identity management built in. ... Enables managers to gather real-time team feedback securely, with built-in templates and analytics that respect data privacy and organizational policies.

At Microsoft, we began deploying Copilot to our own employees back in November of 2023, and by March of 2024 all our employees and vendors globally had access, making Microsoft the first enterprise to deploy Copilot at global enterprise scale. A key lesson we’ve learned is that enterprise AI is a significant cultural and technological change that shouldn’t be underestimated. Consider that knowledge workers have been trained to interact with systems and data using the graphical user interface for over 40 years. The magic of Copilot is that it upends that human-computer interaction paradigm, bringing the power of natural language interaction to employees to find information or answers, to augment their creativity, or to accelerate workflows. But those skills are new for nearly every employee on the planet, which is why you need to focus on skilling and reinforcement to help employees to maximize the potential value of generative AI in the enterprise.

content.title: Upskilling the Workforce: Strategies for 2025 Success
content.text:
Meanwhile, the blockchain market—forecast to expand from USD 26.91 billion in 2024 to USD 1.8 trillion by 2034—is revolutionizing finance, supply chain, and government sectors. For management consultants and strategists, integrating upskilling in these high-growth areas is essential to keep clients competitive and secure in the evolving digital landscape. Many companies keep employee development in-house, leveraging internal subject matter experts and custom, job-specific modules. According to the 2023 Training Industry Report, 43% of L&D professionals create their own development programs, often to ensure close alignment with corporate culture and processes. However, not every skill gap can be addressed internally—especially when facing cutting-edge or highly specialized topics. This is where external partnerships come in. Engaging with universities, professional bodies, or e-learning providers allows organizations to: Access Specialized Expertise Platforms like Coursera or LinkedIn L

Meanwhile, the blockchain market—forecast to expand from USD 26.91 billion in 2024 to USD 1.8 trillion by 2034—is revolutionizing finance, supply chain, and government sectors. For management consultants and strategists, integrating upskilling in these high-growth areas is essential to keep clients competitive and secure in the evolving digital landscape. Many companies keep employee development in-house, leveraging internal subject matter experts and custom, job-specific modules. According to the 2023 Training Industry Report, 43% of L&D professionals create their own development programs, often to ensure close alignment with corporate culture and processes. However, not every skill gap can be addressed internally—especially when facing cutting-edge or highly specialized topics. This is where external partnerships come in. Engaging with universities, professional bodies, or e-learning providers allows organizations to: Access Specialized Expertise Platforms like Coursera or LinkedIn L

In a world where 40% of workers may require up to six months of reskilling and a projected 94% of business leaders expect employees to pick up entirely new skills, upskilling has moved from a nice-to-have option to a critical business priority. According to PwC research, 93% of CEOs who implemented formal upskilling initiatives reported notable gains in productivity, talent retention, and overall workforce resilience. These findings underscore how proactively investing in your existing talent can be far more effective than searching for external hires to fill skill gaps. Aura Data Point: In late 2024, job postings for customer success management surged by 25%, reflecting how businesses now prioritize customer retention and engagement. This shift highlights a broader trend in which upskilled teams—particularly those combining customer-centric and analytical skills—outperform in fast-changing markets.

A common pitfall is treating upskilling as an isolated HR function instead of a strategic initiative. According to LinkedIn’s 2024 Workplace Learning Report, aligning training with overarching business objectives remains the top priority for L&D professionals. When learning solutions directly support an organization’s strategic goals, companies can experience significant boosts in revenue, productivity, and employee retention. In fact, research from the Association for Talent Development (ATD) indicates that organizations with well-aligned training programs can see up to 218% higher income per employee than those with less comprehensive programs. For management consultants overseeing upskilling or reskilling projects, the following questions are critical to establishing that alignment from the start: What are the strategic goals for the next 3–5 years?

L&D units are the architects and drivers of upskilling programs. They design curricula, select delivery formats, and oversee learning management systems (LMS) that track participation and outcomes. This responsibility has far-reaching impact: according to LinkedIn’s 2024 Workplace Learning Report, 70% of workers say that learning opportunities improve their sense of connection to a workplace, underscoring how an effective L&D function can boost retention and engagement. To maximize impact, collaboration among L&D, executive leadership, and line managers is crucial. For instance, 47% of companies invest in coaching and mentoring—practices typically initiated by L&D—to strengthen leadership pipelines and reduce talent shortages. By aligning their learning solutions with organizational strategies and then rallying stakeholders around those initiatives, L&D teams can transform training from a siloed HR effort into a strategic lever for competitive advantage.

좋은 자료들이 모였습니다. 이제 CSP의 2026 미션 4개 축과 직접 연결되는 방식으로 선진기업 사례를 분석해서 제안드리겠습니다.

---

## 선진기업의 AX 내재화 사례 — 그리고 LG PRI가 배울 수 있는 것들

---

### 들어가며: 왜 대부분의 AX는 내재화에 실패하는가

먼저 냉정한 숫자 하나를 직시할 필요가 있습니다. 2025년 McKinsey 보고서에 따르면 92%의 기업이 AI 투자를 늘릴 계획이지만, 실제로 AI가 조직 전반의 측정 가능한 성과를 만들어내는 '완전한 운영 통합' 단계에 도달한 기업은 단 1%에 불과합니다. 이것이 내재화 문제의 본질입니다. 도입은 쉽고, 내재화는 어렵습니다.

IDC 조사에 따르면 전체 AI 프로젝트의 88%가 파일럿 단계에 머물러 전사 규모로 나아가지 못합니다. 33개 AI 이니셔티브 중 성공적으로 확산되는 것은 통계적으로 4개에 불과합니다. 기술의 문제가 아닙니다. 조직과 사람의 문제입니다.

그렇다면 그 1%에 속하는 기업들은 무엇을 다르게 했는가. 그 질문에 답하는 네 가지 사례가 있습니다.

---

### Case 1. Microsoft — "자사가 먼저 쓰는 기업" 전략

Microsoft의 AX 내재화가 설득력을 갖는 이유는 단순합니다. 그들은 Copilot을 판매하기 전에 자신들이 먼저 실험했습니다. Microsoft는 2023년 11월부터 자사 임직원에게 Copilot을 배포하기 시작했고, 2024년 3월까지 전 세계 모든 직원과 협력사에 접근권을 부여했습니다 — 글로벌 엔터프라이즈 규모에서 최초로 Copilot을 전사 배포한 기업이 된 것입니다.

내재화 방법론으로는 학습 생태계 설계가 돋보입니다. Marketing AI practitioner hub를 통해 마케터들이 AI를 꾸준히 실천할 수 있는 정기적인 접점을 만들었고, MCAPS Academy는 영업 인력이 AI 학습에 계속 참여하도록 뉴스레터와 내부 커뮤니케이션을 활용했습니다. 엔지니어링 팀은 The Garage를 통해 AI 프로젝트 성과를 공유하는 Show-and-Tell 세션을 운영했습니다.

이것이 LG PRI에 주는 시사점: 단순 교육이 아니라 '반복 접점'의 설계가 내재화의 핵심입니다. CSP의 Vibe-Coding 교육 6개 과정 신설은 도입부이고, 그 이후에 마케터 허브처럼 실천 커뮤니티가 따라와야 합니다.

---

### Case 2. IKEA — "30,000명을 위한 AI 리터러시 아키텍처"

2024년 출범한 IKEA의 AI 리터러시 이니셔티브는 30,000명의 직원과 500명의 리더를 대상으로 AI 도구를 책임감 있게 활용하는 능력을 기르는 것을 목표로 합니다. 규모만 인상적인 것이 아닙니다. 설계 철학이 다릅니다.

레벨 구조가 정교합니다. 모든 직원을 위한 AI Fundamentals 기초 과정, Responsible AI·Mastering Gen AI·Algorithmic Training for Ethics 등의 전문 프로그램, 리더십 집중 세션인 AI Exploration Days, 그리고 ML/AI 석·박사급 고급 인재를 위한 Accelerator Programme까지 — 단계별로 서로 다른 대상에게 맞춤화된 학습 경로를 제공합니다.

CSP의 "모두의 AX" 트랙 A/B, Lv.1~4 체계와 거의 정확히 동형 구조입니다. IKEA는 이것을 이미 30,000명 규모로 실행했고, 그 경험이 증명하는 것은 레벨 분화 없이 일괄 교육으로는 내재화가 일어나지 않는다는 사실입니다.

---

### Case 3. Airbus — "엔지니어 10,000명의 기술 내재화"

Airbus는 약 10,000명의 엔지니어를 GitHub 등의 도구로 교육하는 데 투자했고, 그 결과 항공기 설계 시뮬레이션 속도가 40% 가속되었습니다. 이 사례가 Physical AI Tech Leader 육성에 주는 함의는 명확합니다. 도구 교육 자체보다 도구를 통해 달성하고자 하는 구체적인 업무 성과물이 먼저 정의되어야 한다는 것입니다. Airbus가 "GitHub 교육"을 한 것이 아니라 "시뮬레이션 속도 향상을 위해 GitHub을 가르친" 것처럼, LG PRI의 Physical AI Tech Leader 육성도 Skill Ontology가 단순 역량 목록이 아니라 특정 현장 문제의 해결 경로로 설계되어야 합니다.

---

### Case 4. BCI (British Columbia Investment Management) — "생산성 측정의 정밀화"

CSP의 KPI인 "생산성 10% 개선"을 달성한 실제 사례가 있습니다. BCI는 Microsoft 365 Copilot과 Azure 생태계를 활용하여 수작업을 자동화한 결과, Copilot 사용자의 84%에서 10~20%의 생산성 향상이 이루어졌고, 직무 만족도는 68% 향상되었습니다. 내부 감사 보고서 작성 시간은 30% 단축되었으며, 8,000개의 설문 코멘트 분석에 한 달이 소요되던 처리 시간이 대폭 줄었습니다.

특히 설문 코멘트 분석 자동화 사례는 CSP의 Pulse Check 시스템과 직결됩니다. 조직 건강도 데이터를 분기에 한 번 수동으로 처리하는 방식이 아니라, AI가 상시적으로 텍스트 응답을 분석하고 패턴을 감지하는 구조로 가야 한다는 것을 BCI 사례는 보여줍니다.

---

### 선진기업 사례를 관통하는 세 가지 내재화 패턴

조각난 사례들을 하나의 프레임으로 통합하면, 성공한 AX 내재화 기업들은 공통적으로 세 가지 조건을 갖추고 있습니다.

**첫 번째는 내부 챔피언 네트워크입니다.** 성공한 조직들은 내부 변화 주도자가 이니셔티브를 이끌게 하고, 모범 사례를 공유하는 커뮤니티 오브 프랙티스를 만들며, 도입 성과를 게임처럼 경쟁하게 하고, 초기 성과를 기념합니다. 상명하달식 롤아웃이 아니라 내부 전파자가 스스로 동료를 설득하는 구조입니다.

**두 번째는 측정 가능한 성과 연결입니다.** AI 상위 성과 기업들 — AI 활용으로 EBIT의 5% 이상을 창출하는 조직들 — 은 대담한 목표, 워크플로우 재설계, 빠른 확산, 그리고 동종 기업보다 현저히 높은 AI 역량 투자라는 공통 특성을 보입니다.

**세 번째는 심리적 안전감의 선행 구축입니다.** PwC와 WEF 공동 보고서에 따르면 조기 채택자들은 더 넓은 실행에 앞서 소규모 그룹에서 GenAI 솔루션을 개발하고 테스트하는 것의 중요성을 강조했으며, 리스크 관리와 '인간 참여 프로세스'에 집중했습니다. 기술 도입 전에 "이것은 당신의 역할을 위협하지 않는다"는 신뢰 기반이 먼저 만들어져야 내재화가 일어납니다.

---

### LG PRI에 대한 제언

이 모든 사례를 종합하면, LG PRI의 AX 내재화 전략에 다음 세 가지를 보완 제안합니다.

**Pulse Check에 AI 기반 텍스트 분석 레이어 추가**: BCI 사례처럼, 설문 응답의 정량 집계를 넘어 자유응답 텍스트에서 패턴을 추출하는 AI 레이어를 설계하면 "Well-Being 15% 개선"이라는 KPI를 단순 점수 변화가 아니라 원인 진단 기반의 개입으로 연결할 수 있습니다.

**AX 챔피언 네트워크의 공식화**: Microsoft와 IKEA 모두 내부 커뮤니티 오브 프랙티스를 공식 제도로 운영합니다. Vibe-Coding 교육 이수자 중 우수자를 단순 수료생이 아닌 AX 챔피언으로 공식 임명하고, 이들이 팀 단위 전파자로 기능하게 설계하면 교육 효과의 반감기를 늦출 수 있습니다.

**Physical AI Tech Leader 육성의 성과 중심 재정의**: Airbus처럼, "Lv.3 이상 30명 Pool"이라는 수량 목표를 "특정 현장 문제를 AI로 해결한 경험이 있는 엔지니어 30명"으로 재정의하면 그 자체가 내부 레퍼런스 스토리가 되고 이후 확산의 기반이 됩니다.

내재화란 결국 기술이 일상 언어가 되는 과정입니다. 사람들이 AI를 사용하는 것이 특별한 일이 아닌, 당연한 업무 방식이 되는 그 임계점까지 가는 것 — 선진기업들의 사례가 공통으로 향하는 방향이 그것입니다.

좋습니다. 이제 인프라·도구 지원과 내재화를 연결하는 논리 구조가 선명하게 보입니다. LG PRI의 M365 환경을 기반으로 직접 연결되는 제안을 드리겠습니다.

---

## 인프라 및 도구 지원과 연계한 AX 내재화 방법론

---

### 전제: 왜 도구만으로는 내재화가 일어나지 않는가

인프라와 도구를 도입하면 내재화가 따라온다는 것은 가장 흔한 착각입니다. 직원들이 AI를 이해하지 못하거나, 신뢰하지 않거나, 가치를 체감하지 못하면 기술적으로 아무리 정교한 구현이 이루어져도 채택률은 바닥을 친다 — 조직들은 AI 시스템 구축과 배포에 막대한 투자를 하고도, 직원들이 익숙한 수동 프로세스를 계속 사용하는 것을 지켜보게 된다.

도구는 가능성의 공간을 만듭니다. 내재화는 그 공간 안에서 사람이 움직이는 방식을 설계하는 일입니다. 따라서 인프라·도구와 내재화를 연결하는 올바른 질문은 "어떤 도구를 도입할 것인가"가 아니라 "도구가 일상 업무 흐름 안에 어떻게 착지할 것인가"입니다.

선진기업들이 이 문제를 어떻게 풀었는지, 세 가지 구조적 패턴으로 정리하면 다음과 같습니다.

---

### 패턴 1. CoE(Center of Excellence) — 도구 거버넌스의 중추 신경

AI CoE는 전사 AI 전략, 거버넌스, 역량 지원을 위한 중앙 허브 역할을 하는 크로스펑셔널 팀입니다. 부서별로 AI 이니셔티브가 단절된 상태로 발전하는 것을 막고, 조율된 가이던스, 표준, 지원을 제공합니다. 이것을 CSP가 즐겨 쓰는 비유로 바꾸면 — CoE는 건축 현장에서 각 팀이 제각각 공사하지 않도록 설계도를 쥔 건축 사무소입니다.

CoE 없이 도구만 배포했을 때 무슨 일이 벌어지는지 실제 사례가 있습니다. 글로벌 제조기업에서 영업팀은 Azure OpenAI로 고객 챗봇을 만들고, HR 부서는 Copilot Studio로 직원 문의 시스템을 만들고, 운영팀은 Azure ML로 예측 유지보수 모델을 개발했는데 — 모두 독립적으로. 그 결과 마케팅과 고객 서비스팀이 각각 유사한 감성 분석 솔루션을 구축했고, Azure 구독은 중복되었으며 시스템 간 인사이트 공유는 불가능했다.

CoE의 구성 요소로는 전략·거버넌스·기술 지원·지식 공유·인재 개발 다섯 기능이 있으며, 실행 스폰서(Executive Sponsor)가 비전을 챔피언하고 예산을 확보하는 역할을 담당합니다.

**LG PRI 적용 관점**: EXG 팀이 자연스럽게 이 CoE 역할을 맡을 수 있습니다. 다만 "HR CoE"가 아니라 "AX CoE"로 포지셔닝해야 합니다. 교육을 설계하는 팀이 아니라, 전사 AX 도구 표준과 내재화 속도를 관리하는 팀으로.

---

### 패턴 2. 도구 접근성의 계층 설계 — "쓰게 만드는" 인프라

도구가 아무리 좋아도 접근 장벽이 높으면 사람들은 쓰지 않습니다. 선진기업들이 이 문제를 해결한 방식은 도구를 업무 흐름 안에 직접 내장하는 것입니다.

Microsoft의 자사 배포 경험이 이 지점에서 가장 구체적입니다. 단순한 에이전트의 경우 직원들이 Copilot Chat이나 Teams 내에서 직접 제작할 수 있게 하고, 부서 수준의 복잡한 에이전트는 Copilot Studio를 사용하며, 전사 수준의 엔터프라이즈 에이전트는 Azure AI Foundry를 통해 정규 소프트웨어 개발 라이프사이클을 적용한다. 이것은 도구의 위계가 아니라 사용자 역량의 위계입니다. 누구나 자신의 수준에서 시작할 수 있도록 진입 계단을 설계한 것입니다.

도구 채택의 물리적 마찰을 줄이는 방법도 중요합니다. AI 관리자들은 가장 가치 있는 에이전트를 전체 조직을 위해 중앙에서 고정(pinning)할 수 있게 되었으며, 기능별 에이전트를 특정 사용자 그룹에 맞춤 배치하여 팀이 더 빠르게 시작하고 집중할 수 있도록 지원한다. 사람들이 도구를 '찾을' 필요 없이 일하다 보면 '만나게' 되는 설계입니다.

Accenture는 Copilot Studio와 Power Platform으로 CoE 팀을 확장하여 연간 상당한 비용을 절감했으며, 5만 명 이상의 시민 개발자(citizen developer)를 새롭게 육성하고 IT 부서의 단기 애플리케이션 수요를 30% 줄여 전문 개발자들이 더 복잡한 프로젝트에 집중할 수 있게 했습니다.

**LG PRI 적용 관점**: Vibe-Coding 교육의 핵심 성과물이 "AI 활용 능력이 있는 사람"이 아니라 "자기 업무용 도구를 직접 만들 수 있는 시민 개발자"여야 합니다. Lovable이나 Power Automate로 본인 업무 워크플로우를 자동화하는 경험 자체가 내재화의 증거가 됩니다.

---

### 패턴 3. 측정 인프라 — 내재화를 가시화하는 대시보드

내재화가 진행 중인지 정체 중인지를 알아야 개입이 가능합니다. 이것이 바로 "측정 인프라"입니다.

정기적인 서비스 건강 리뷰(Service Health Review)에서 구현팀과 비즈니스 이해관계자들이 Microsoft 365 Admin Center와 Copilot Dashboard 데이터를 기반으로 AI 전환 프로젝트의 인사이트를 검토하고, 개선 기회를 논의하며 행동 지향적 라운드테이블 세션에서 우선순위를 정한다.

단순 사용량 지표를 넘어선 측정도 있습니다. BCI는 Copilot 사용자의 84%에서 생산성 향상을 확인했고, 내부 감사 보고서 작성 시간을 30% 단축했으며, 8,000개의 설문 코멘트 분석에 소요되던 한 달의 처리 시간을 자동화로 대폭 줄였습니다. 이처럼 성과 지표는 "몇 명이 사용했는가"가 아니라 "어떤 업무 시간이 얼마나 줄었는가"로 설정되어야 합니다.

**LG PRI 적용 관점**: CSP의 KPI인 "생산성 10% 개선"을 측정하는 방법 자체가 내재화 설계의 일부입니다. Pulse Check 시스템에 AI 도구 활용 빈도·질·업무 시간 변화를 묻는 문항을 넣거나, 워크플로우 자동화로 절약된 시간을 정량화하는 메커니즘을 초기부터 설계해야 합니다.

---

### LG PRI를 위한 통합 내재화 프레임워크

위의 세 패턴을 LG PRI 맥락에서 하나의 흐름으로 연결하면 다음과 같은 구조가 됩니다.

**1단계 — 접근 환경 정비 (인프라 레이어)**
M365 Copilot 라이선스 현황, SharePoint 데이터 거버넌스, Power Automate 접근 권한을 정비합니다. AI가 유용하려면 데이터가 갖춰져야 합니다 — AI 플랫폼이 ERP, 내부 데이터베이스, 서드파티 API에서 구조적·비구조적 데이터를 집계하고 처리할 수 있어야 합니다. LG PRI의 경우 인사 데이터, Pulse Check 응답 데이터, ESCON의 스킬 데이터가 각각 연결 가능한 형태로 정비되어 있는지가 전제 조건입니다.

**2단계 — CoE 공식화 (거버넌스 레이어)**
EXG 팀 중심의 AX CoE를 구성합니다. CoE는 AI 채택이 의도적이고, 안전하며, 비즈니스 목표와 정렬되도록 보장합니다 — 단순히 흥미롭게 들리지만 첫 번째 장벽도 통과하지 못하는 기술이 되지 않도록. CoE의 핵심 산출물은 사용 가이드라인, 도구별 유스케이스 라이브러리, 성과 측정 기준입니다.

**3단계 — 챔피언 네트워크 가동 (전파 레이어)**
Vibe-Coding 교육 이수자 중 우수자를 "AX Champion"으로 공식 임명합니다. 챔피언을 선발하세요. 챔피언은 비즈니스 사용자들이 Copilot을 사용하는 것을 도울 것입니다. 이들은 교육의 수혜자가 아니라 동료 설득의 주체가 됩니다. 심리학적으로 말하면, 권위자의 설득보다 동료의 실천 사례가 행동 변화를 이끌어내는 데 훨씬 강력합니다.

**4단계 — 성과 가시화 루프 (측정 레이어)**
작은 그룹으로 시작하고, 성과를 측정하고, 반복하고, 확장하세요. AI 채택은 의도적으로 성장할 때 가장 잘 작동합니다. 매 분기 Pulse Check가 단순한 웰빙 측정이 아니라 AX 내재화 성숙도 측정 도구도 겸하도록 설계하면, 조직 건강도와 AX 진척도를 하나의 데이터 생태계에서 관리할 수 있습니다.

---

### 마지막으로

인프라와 도구는 무대를 세우는 일입니다. 내재화는 그 무대 위에서 사람들이 자연스럽게 연기를 시작하게 만드는 일입니다. AI를 직원들의 반대와 기술에 대한 신뢰 부족으로 인해 방해받지 않으려면, AI를 대체가 아닌 증강으로 포지셔닝하는 포괄적 변화 관리, 교육 프로그램, 문화적 전환이 필요합니다.

기술이 준비되어 있어도 사람이 준비되지 않으면 내재화는 일어나지 않습니다. 반대로 사람이 준비되어 있어도 도구 접근 환경이 불편하면 채택이 지속되지 않습니다. 이 두 조건이 동시에 설계될 때 비로소 AX는 "프로그램"이 아닌 "일하는 방식"이 됩니다.

content.title: AI Infrastructure 2025: Complete Guide & Best Practices
content.text:
For hybrid or on-premises deployments, implement container orchestration using Kubernetes to enable portable, scalable application deployment. Configure infrastructure-as-code using tools like Terraform or Pulumi to ensure reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure configurations. Establish CI/CD pipelines specifically designed for machine learning, incorporating automated testing, model validation, and deployment workflows. Integration connects AI infrastructure components with existing enterprise systems including data warehouses, business applications, identity management, and monitoring platforms. Design integration points with security in mind, exposing AI models through secure API gateways with rate limiting, input validation, and authentication using OAuth2 or JWT tokens. Swedish video game developer Mojang Studios faced the challenge of optimizing content recommendations in the Minecraft Marketplace while enhancing personalization for millions of players.

In 2025, effective AI infrastructure combines specialized compute resources, robust data management systems, and automated deployment pipelines to support increasingly complex machine learning workloads. Market Growth: The AI infrastructure market is projected to reach $499 billion by 2034, driven by enterprise adoption and generative AI expansion · Investment Patterns: Hyperscalers are investing over $100 billion annually, while 70% of organizations allocate at least 10% of IT budgets to AI initiatives · Implementation Reality: 80% of enterprises miss AI infrastructure spending forecasts by 25% or more · ROI Potential: Companies deploying AI infrastructure with strong execution strategies achieve returns between 150% and 350% Adoption Patterns: 98% of enterprises favor hybrid architectures combining on-premises, cloud, and edge deployments · AI infrastructure refers to the integrated hardware, software, and network resources that enable organizations to build, train, and operate artif

In 2025, AI infrastructure has become critical infrastructure. Organizations report that 78% now use AI in operations, up from 55% the previous year, marking mainstream adoption across industries. This rapid acceleration creates competitive pressure—companies with optimized AI infrastructure deploy models faster, iterate more frequently, and capture market opportunities while competitors remain stuck in prototyping phases. A mid-market enterprise software company demonstrates the tangible impact of well-designed AI infrastructure. Facing challenges with fragmented sales data across multiple systems, they implemented an AI-powered deal intelligence platform integrated directly with their Salesforce CRM. The technical challenge centered on unifying disparate data sources—customer interactions, deal progress, and market trends—into a cohesive system providing real-time intelligence.

For hybrid or on-premises deployments, implement container orchestration using Kubernetes to enable portable, scalable application deployment. Configure infrastructure-as-code using tools like Terraform or Pulumi to ensure reproducible, version-controlled infrastructure configurations. Establish CI/CD pipelines specifically designed for machine learning, incorporating automated testing, model validation, and deployment workflows. Integration connects AI infrastructure components with existing enterprise systems including data warehouses, business applications, identity management, and monitoring platforms. Design integration points with security in mind, exposing AI models through secure API gateways with rate limiting, input validation, and authentication using OAuth2 or JWT tokens. Organizations invest heavily in developing and deploying AI systems, only to watch them gather dust as employees continue using familiar manual processes. content.text:
Here's the thing: successful enterprise AI integration with existing systems requires organizations to implement proper data management, model training infrastructure, central model registry, model deployment practices, and ongoing monitoring.

The International Energy Agency reports that global data center electricity consumption could rise to over 1,000 TWh by 2026. Any AI infrastructure decisions must balance performance needs with energy efficiency and sustainability goals. Building for AI means thinking about adaptability - designing environments that support the data gravity, model training, and dynamic access needs of AI operations. The key is honest assessment. Most organizations can make AI work with their existing infrastructure, but only if they're realistic about what needs to change. I get it - you've got decades of technology that wasn't built for AI, and now you're supposed to make it all work together. But that doesn't mean you're stuck. Most traditional frameworks simply can't support the kind of processing power and data flow that AI requires. You're dealing with outdated technologies that were never designed to talk to modern AI solutions.

You need a resourcing plan that adapts to your use cases, including whether you'll build capabilities internally or bring in external expertise. Most successful organizations start with a community of practice that brings together stakeholders interested in AI, then evolve into dedicated teams focused on high-priority activities. Before you do anything, define your business objectives. Obvious, isn’t it? But you'd be surprised how many AI projects start with "let's try this cool technology" instead of "let's solve this specific business problem." Identify the challenges where AI can make a real difference. Set realistic goals around customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, or revenue growth. Take inventory of your current technology landscape to understand where AI fits naturally. ... Business teams often demand AI capabilities that are impossible with current infrastructure.

While technical challenges like budget constraints and legacy infrastructure limitations exist, they're not the core issue. I've witnessed firsthand how even well-funded projects stumble. The fundamental problems run deeper. Poor data quality destroys AI value from day one. Inconsistent or incomplete datasets render sophisticated AI models useless. Combine this with the complexity of connecting generative AI to legacy systems lacking proper APIs, and you have a perfect storm for expensive failures. This explains why enterprise AI integration with existing systems usually runs into delays and disappointing results. The difference between successful AI integration and another failed initiative usually comes down to execution. This isn’t about more funding or better models - it’s about knowing where to plug AI into the systems you already run. You’ll learn how to assess infrastructure readiness, map the right connection points, and implement performance monitoring that proves AI’s value i

Here's the thing: successful enterprise AI integration with existing systems requires organizations to implement proper data management, model training infrastructure, central model registry, model deployment practices, and ongoing monitoring. These components ensure that AI systems remain reliable, accurate, and relevant as business needs evolve. AI isn't just about staying current with tech trends. Let me walk you through what actually happens when AI integration works. The biggest win from AI integration? It eliminates the repetitive tasks that eat up your team's day. In manufacturing, AI predicts machine failures before they happen, cutting downtime and maintenance costs. In healthcare, AI scans handwritten documents and converts them to editable text, centralizing patient information that was previously scattered. But the real value isn't just automation.

MIT’s recent research found that 95% of generative AI pilots at companies are failing (Fortune, 2025). That’s a massive gap between trying AI and actually making it work. Here’s an important lesson from companies excelling at AI integration for enterprises: they didn’t rush the process. They dedicated months, sometimes over a year, carefully refining their data architecture before rolling out AI solutions. Take this retail example. Company A invested heavily in generative AI integration for enterprises to automate customer service, but struggled because their data formats were inconsistent across different regions. Meanwhile, Company B took eighteen months to standardize its data infrastructure first. The result? They saw a 40% improvement in customer satisfaction scores within six months of AI deployment.

Here’s a breakdown of the enterprise AI integration strategy you can follow: Before implementing AI, your first step should be checking out your existing infrastructure. Here’s a cautionary tale that’ll make you think twice. A Fortune 500 manufacturing giant threw $15 million at an AI-powered predictive maintenance system. Sounds smart, right? Well, six months later, they discovered their legacy infrastructure couldn’t handle real-time data processing. The project was shelved, not because AI failed, but because integration wasn’t planned from the ground up. This is not just a fictional story but the reality for many. According to RAND, over 80% of AI projects fail twice the rate of regular IT projects (RAND, 2024). The culprit? Rushing into AI without building the right foundations. Thankfully, the scenario is shifting now. Enterprises are moving from “let’s do AI because it’s hot” to “let’s use AI to solve real business challenges.” Understanding how AI can address your unique business needs is the first step in preparing your enterprise AI integration readiness strategy.

content.text:
Start with a small group, measure outcomes, iterate, and expand. AI adoption works best when it grows deliberately. ... Get Ready → Onboard & Engage → Deliver Impact → Extend & Optimize. Good enablement is half the battle.

A well-built Center of Excellence (CoE) gives you exactly that, a clear strategy, smart guardrails, and a team that ensures your people get the most out of Copilot from day one, not left guessing how to use it. A CoE ensures Copilot adoption is intentional, secure, and aligned with your business goals, not just a new piece of tech that sounds exciting but never makes it past the first hurdle. Copilot is reshaping how people write, analyze, meet, and collaborate. But successful adoption isn’t automatic. It takes governance, user enablement, and a culture that understands how to use AI responsibly. ... Think of it as your AI nerve center: strategy, governance, and enablement all working together. You don’t need a cast of thousands, just a cross-functional team with clearly defined responsibilities. We’d recommend: Executive Sponsor – Champions the vision and secures funding.

Start with a small group, measure outcomes, iterate, and expand. AI adoption works best when it grows deliberately. ... Get Ready → Onboard & Engage → Deliver Impact → Extend & Optimize. Good enablement is half the battle. Create an AI “innovation lab” so teams can test new Copilot scenarios safely and share wins. Audit your AI systems for transparency, fairness, and responsible use. Governance shouldn’t feel restrictive, it should feel empowering. Create a central hub where employees can find best practices, examples, and success stories. Think of it as your Copilot playbook. ... These early wins build trust and momentum. A strong Copilot CoE helps you move from experimentation to real impact, securely, responsibly, and at a pace that fits your organization. If you’re ready to build or refine your CoE, we’re here to help you accelerate that journey.

content.text:
For more complex agents intended to meet enterprise needs across lines of business or the company as a whole, our developers use more full-featured tools like Copilot Studio or Azure AI Foundry. For these kinds of agents, we apply the same rigor, reviews, and software development lifecycle (SDL) we use as part of our standard internal app development. As you explore the different kinds of agents available to your users and decide how and where to enable them, adoption.microsoft.com provides an excellent place to start. It provides three different approaches to creating agents: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, Azure AI Foundry, and Copilot Studio. All of this choice adds complexity, so maintaining visibility and control over the agents your employees create can be a challenge. As a result, we take a matrixed approach to creating and governing agents based on different parameters.

This chapter outlines the highly robust, best-case scenario we created for Microsoft, but we know not every organization has a fully deployed data governance strategy. If you’re in that position, don’t worry! You can use Restricted SharePoint Search to provide instant value and protection without exposing Copilot to all of your internal SharePoint sites. We’ve developed four data labeling practices that make up our foundation for appropriate policies and settings. Enable your employees to create new workspaces like SharePoint sites, ensuring your company data is on your Microsoft 365 tenant. That enables your people to take full advantage of Copilot in ways that align with your organizational data hygiene while you keep your company’s information safe. Label containers for data segmentation by default to ensure your information isn’t overexposed.

Controlling the flow Limit oversharing at the source by enabling company-shareable links instead of forcing employees to grant access to large groups. To enforce these behaviors, you can set default link types based on labels through Purview. Oversharing detection Even under the best circumstances, accidents happen. When one of our employees does overshare sensitive data, we use Microsoft Graph Data Connect extraction in conjunction with Microsoft Purview to catch and report oversharing. Europe has extra requirements in the form of EU Data Boundary regulations and works councils, organizations that provide employee co-determination on workers’ rights or regulatory issues. content.title: New capabilities for AI admins from Ignite 2025 | Microsoft Community Hub
content.text:
This streamlined pinning experience ... you can utilize the agent enablement controls, now available under Agents > Settings in the Microsoft 365 admin center....

With completion status, user coverage insights, and clear explanations for each control, admins can confidently plan, sequence, and implement Microsoft 365 Copilot in a more coherent and structured way. It also surfaces new or recommended actions, like enabling image generation, with simple guidance that helps ensure users get the full value of Copilot features, and what they expect from modern AI experiences. Organizational messages in the Microsoft 365 admin center helps IT teams drive Copilot and agent awareness and adoption by reaching users through the Microsoft products they use every day. Since the GA release of Organizational messages at Ignite last year, we’ve heard two important themes from you: 1) a need to more intelligently target users based on activity and 2) support for email as communication channel. At Ignite 2025, we announced two key enhancements planned for general availability in Q1 2026: Action segments (Preview): with Action segments, target messages based on us

Email delivery channel (Preview): Organizational messages can now be delivered via email, alongside Windows Taskbar, Windows notifications, Spotlight and Teams popover messages. With email as a delivery channel, admins can send premade email templates, such as “Welcome to Copilot” and weekly “Great M365 Copilot Journey” mails, to boost adoption. Note that the preview experience includes only templatized messages, and customizable emails are planned for GA in March 2026. Enabling email in organizational messages allows admins to communicate with users through familiar channels, driving awareness and ensuring consistent messaging across devices. Microsoft Agent 365 is the new control plane that enables organizations to deploy, organize and govern agents at scale, complementing the capabilities CCS provides for Microsoft 365 Copilot. It integrates identity, compliance, and security from Microsoft Entra, and Defender, and provides unified observability through dashboards and alerts.

Pinning agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot gives people instant access to the most relevant AI capabilities for their roles, whether that’s a Sales coach agent for sellers or a workflow‑specific agent for Finance. With new admin agent pinning controls, now available in the Microsoft 365 admin center, AI administrators can now centrally curate and pin high‑value agents for the entire organization. Admins can also tailor the experience by pinning function‑specific agents for targeted users or security groups, helping teams start faster, stay focused, and get more done. This streamlined pinning experience empowers IT to guide adoption, highlight strategic agents, and make agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot more useful and discoverable from day one. To help you manage agent adoption responsibility, you can utilize the agent enablement controls, now available under Agents > Settings in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

By clicking on an MCP server-enabled agent, admins can navigate to a dedicated details section that surfaces the server’s URL, authentication type, and dynamic/static status. As MCP server agents become a core part of the Copilot ecosystem, this visibility helps AI admins continue to drive agent adoption securely and at scale. Cloud Update, now generally available, modernizes how organizations manage Microsoft 365 Apps by automating updates and ensuring devices stay current, secure, and consistent. This cloud‑driven servicing model is especially important for Microsoft 365 Copilot and agents because maintaining high update currency ensures they run on optimized, secure, and feature‑ready environments. By reducing operational overhead through automated monthly service, admins can focus less on manual maintenance and more on delivering the latest AI experiences.
